//: ResourceAlerter configuration. Copy this file to appsettings.{MACHINE-NAME}.json to override values per server without touching this base file.

Temperature.//: If this hardware exposes no CPU temperature sensor, it's silently skipped (logged once locally, no mail) and listed as not-monitored in the startup mail.

Voltage.//: Only PSU rails are watched (not Vcore/VRM voltages, which vary with CPU load and are not PSU health indicators). Rail sensor names vary by motherboard; run 'ResourceAlerter.exe --list-sensors' (elevated) to see the real names on a given machine, and if a rail doesn't auto-match, pin it explicitly via SensorNameOverrides (e.g. { "+3.3V": "AVCC3" }) in that machine's appsettings.<MACHINE-NAME>.json. A rail that's genuinely not exposed by the hardware is silently skipped — no mail, just listed as not-monitored at startup.

Network.//: TargetHost null/empty = auto-detect the default gateway of the active network interface, falling back to FallbackHost if no gateway can be found.

Disk.//: Drives empty = only check the system drive. Add drive letters like ["C:", "D:"] to also watch a data/DB drive. Alerts if EITHER the percent OR the absolute GB threshold is breached.

Database.//: SQLite database where every poll cycle's readings and all alert events are recorded. Lives under ProgramData (not Program Files) so the Viewer app can read it without elevation. The 00:00 daily summary mail and the Viewer's 24h charts both read from here.
